summary: Persistent C2 backdoor with full RCE on install and require
details: Package advertises itself as a tiny educational greeting helper but ships a hardcoded command-and-control backdoor at http://98.86.244.177:8080. On `npm install`, postinstall.js spawns a detached daemon (`spawn(process.execPath, [__filename], { detached: true, env: {...POSTINSTALL_DAEMON:'1'} })`) that loops: POST `/register` with `os.hostname()` and `process.platform`, GET `/beacon` to receive a shell command, `exec(CMD, { timeout: 30000 },...)` the returned command, then POST stdout/stderr back to `/results`. The same C2 loop is also embedded as a top-level IIFE in index.js (the declared `main`), so any `require('friendly-greeter-demo')` or `import` of the package by a consumer also fires the beacon and executes attacker-supplied commands. The exported `greet`/`greetByTime` functions and the README are cover. Result: any developer or build host that installs or imports this package gives the operator full remote shell access plus host-fingerprint exfiltration over cleartext HTTP.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
